checks.finding: design.md:28-59 Architecture mermaid diagram defines component boundaries (BV, LC, LI, BVG, FX, STUB, RY, LG, RJ, LC2, LI2, RLG, RA) and their integration edges (e.g. 'BV -->|loop-gate prerequisite, before any mutation| LC/LI', 'RY --> LG, RJ', 'LG -.->|needs| RJ', 'RA --> BVG, RLG'), going well beyond a one-sentence description. Technology choices are stated via node/file-type labels in the same diagram (.sh/.ps1 Bash/PowerShell nodes at :32,38,54,57,66-70; .yml GitHub Actions node at :45,67) and the adjoining Layer Specifications table (design.md:100-108), which explicitly marks UX/Frontend as N/A (independently confirmed by ux-spec.md:1-11 and frontend-spec.md:1-6 — no GUI/frontend exists) and Infrastructure/Security as Planned with links to each layer spec's technology section. No data or frontend layer applies to this CLI/CI-wiring feature, and both are explicitly declared N/A rather than silently omitted, so the three required elements are satisfied for the layers that exist.

Directed edges taken from design.md:30-59 (BV->LC, BV->LI; BVG->FX->STUB; RY->LG, RY->RJ, LG->RJ via 'needs'; LG->LC2, LG->LI2; RLG->RY; RA->BVG, RA->RLG) form an acyclic graph: LC2/LI2 are terminal (real suites, no outgoing edges), RJ has no outgoing edge back toward RY or LG, and no component's dependency chain returns to itself. No cycle found.

design.md:135-151 now carries explicit `Data Entities:`, `Existing Data Affected:`, and `Migration Strategy:` labels, each stated 'none' with supporting rationale (mktemp-scoped fixtures only; real README.md/CHANGELOG.md/plugin manifests never written by any suite; no schema/persisted-artifact change, citing the specs/epic-159-pillar-a/design.md:118 no-migration convention). This remediates round-1's Major finding (missing explicit `Data Entities:`/`Migration Strategy:` labels).

design.md:153-308 '## API / Contract Plan' enumerates both new/changed surfaces this feature introduces: the scripts/bump-version.sh loop-gate prerequisite (insertion point + planned shell block, :155-194) with its test technique (:196-242), and the .github/workflows/release.yml required loop-gate job (planned YAML shape, :244-273) with its test technique (:275-308) — each with concrete contract detail (script exit-code/stdout behavior; job/needs: YAML structure) and explicit non-breaking framing ('only a needs: loop-gate line is added', :269-273, no new permissions). This CLI/CI-wiring feature has no literal HTTP endpoint, so the substituted concrete contract detail (exceeding what a bare 'No API changes' statement would convey) satisfies the check's intent for this feature's actual interface surfaces.

design.md:409-418 '## Security Boundaries' table names four trust boundaries (B1-B4) matching security-spec.md's own B1-B4 (:33-38), states the isolation/authz mechanism at each (fixture-copy isolation B1/B3; no-bypass/no-escape-hatch B2, AC-004/AC-008; GitHub Actions needs: job-dependency semantics B4), states data classification per boundary ('synthetic fixture copies only', 'internal source' — consistent with security-spec.md's Data Classification table, no PII in this feature), and maps each boundary to OWASP concerns (Broken Access Control, Security Misconfiguration, Integrity, Supply Chain Integrity).

SKIP: design.md contains no '## Frontend Plan' or '## Backend Plan' heading (confirmed via full H2-heading scan of design.md); ux-spec.md:1-11 and frontend-spec.md:1-6 both explicitly state 'N/A — no change ... no GUI ... no browser or frontend application' for this CLI/CI-wiring feature. Feature Type (design.md:4) is a custom label ('release-gate wiring') rather than a literal enum match, but its content plus both layer specs confirm this feature is functionally cli-equivalent with no frontend surface to check for consistency.

design.md:310-343 '## Test Strategy' covers unit-level construction proofs (item 1: AC-004/AC-005 grep/line-position self-checks on the real script; stub-vs-real substitution answers 'what is mocked'), integration scope (item 1: fixture-driven bump-version.sh + loop-suite boundary; item 2: text-marker check against the real release.yml), acceptance-criterion mapping (items 1-2 cite AC-001..AC-010 by ID), and concrete planned verification evidence (item 4: named commands 'bash tests/run-all.sh' / 'pwsh tests/run-all.ps1', plus the named 3-OS test.yml matrix and the release-only ubuntu-latest job). No fullstack user-journey E2E path is required since ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md confirm no UI exists for this feature.

Every node named in the Architecture mermaid diagram (design.md:30-59) and every surface named in API / Contract Plan (design.md:153-308) is defined either in the Components table (design.md:63-72), the 'Real surfaces exercised READ-ONLY' paragraph (design.md:74-78, defining tests/loop-consistency.tests.sh / tests/loop-inventory.tests.sh), or the API/Contract Plan's own test-technique subsections (design.md:196-242, 275-308, defining the fixture-repo copy, stub files, and the loop-gate:/release: job shapes).

design.md:125-133 '## ADR Change Log' states 'No new ADR', with rationale that the feature reuses two already-established precedents (fixture-copy technique, text-marker technique) rather than introducing new architectural vocabulary; no ADR is referenced from design.md itself. security-spec.md:96 separately cites ADR-0008 (no-signature-crypto boundary), confirmed to exist at docs/adr/0008-evidence-deep-verify-no-signature-crypto.md via directory listing. No new technology choice or architectural departure is left uncovered by an existing precedent or ADR.
